title: Birth-relations --dryRun preview is orientation-only while the live run endpoint-gates the counter-endpoint -- a preview accepts an edge the live filing rejects
description: At filing this gap's premise held that append-block-item / upsert-block-item --dryRun previews a filing's birth relations through the orientation/registration checks only; the implementation investigation corrected the append arm's actual base state — append --dryRun was a CLI-side item-only replica that previewed --relations not at all (and refused schema-absent blocks the live append accepts), while the orientation-only preview existed on upsert's dryRun arm alone. Both arms shared the operative divergence: the live run routes each birth edge through the gated appendRelationByRef, which enforces full write-time reference integrity — the counter-endpoint (the OTHER, already-existing item the edge names) must resolve, and the prospective edge set must stay acyclic — so a --dryRun preview reported would-file for a filing whose birth edge names a dangling counter-endpoint or closes a cycle, outcomes the live run rejects. The new-item endpoint itself is legitimately un-checkable at preview (unwritten by definition, and the live run appends birth edges after the item write so it resolves active); the divergence is confined to the counter-endpoint's existence and the cycle check, both computable read-only at preview time against the existing substrate. Class: exact dry-run outcome preview — the same preview-vs-live divergence class FGAP-066 (closed) fixed for update's catalog-ahead arm by computing the outcome read-only. Known residual instances of the class (new-item endpoint-kind self-skip; the write-pipeline delta-scoped invariant gate running live-only; the upsert item-arm's narrower item-level coverage) are tracked as their own filing, not by this gap.

evidence.reference: the filing ops' dryRun birth-relations branch runs the role-mapping + orientation-ambiguity guard over the entries, registry-only, with endpoint resolution deliberately out (the item is unwritten); the live branch appends the item then routes each birth edge through the gated appendRelationByRef
assertEdgesValidForWrite: the live gate the preview does not model -- counter-endpoint resolution (dangling/unregistered reject) and the prospective-cycle check
the grounding for the write-time parity change whose implementer surfaced this preview/live divergence as an out-of-scope observation at delivery

impact: An operator previewing a filing with birth relations can be told it would file when the live run will refuse it -- the preview is optimistic exactly for the broken-edge cases the write gate exists to catch, sending the operator into a live failure the preview claimed away.
proposed_resolution: Under --dryRun, additionally check each birth-relation entry's counter-endpoint read-only against the existing substrate (resolve it with the same resolver the live gate uses; report a would-reject for dangling/unregistered) and run the prospective-cycle check treating the new item's endpoint as resolving-by-construction -- mirroring the fixed preview pattern the update op's catalog-ahead arm uses (compute the precise outcome read-only, write nothing). The new-item endpoint itself stays exempt from existence checking at preview.
